title: tetraclea coulteri var. angustifolia
intro: Tetraclea coulteri var. angustifolia, also known as Coulter's forest trumpet, is a plant species within the family Gentianaceae, native to California in the United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Tetraclea coulteri var. angustifolia include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic and a diuretic. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which allow for its medicinal properties to be utilized and its health benefits to be harnessed.
